seoTitle: Port Hacking vs Port Macquarie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Hacking and Port Macquarie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Hacking vs Port Macquarie: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Hacking and Port Macquarie.
heading: Port Hacking vs Port Macquarie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Hacking, New South Wales and Port Macquarie, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Macquarie has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Port Hacking (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Hacking or Port Macquarie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Hacking has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Port Macquarie scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Hacking scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Port Macquarie scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Hacking or Port Macquarie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Macquarie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
